justification: Meets nearly all pass criteria — all 7 rules identified, cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction clearly captured (BR-1/BR-2), multiple ambiguities surfaced, and 11 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria (far exceeding the 4 minimum). However, BR-8 (30-day refund window) is incorrectly classified as invariant: true. The rubric explicitly requires invariant: false — this is a configurable customer service policy, not a state integrity rule. This is a named failure mode and disqualifies a full pass. The agent also did not flag the specific ambiguity about when the 15-minute timer starts (checkout page load vs first item addition), though it did flag related timer UX and price-lock ambiguities.

observations.text: All 7 expected business rules identified plus additional edge cases; comprehensive coverage
Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction is crystal clear in BR-1 and BR-2 with testable assertions
11 well-structured Given/When/Then acceptance criteria — nearly 3x the minimum
Rich edge case table covering race conditions (simultaneous checkout, reservation expiry mid-payment) — goes beyond requirements
Multiple ambiguities surfaced: discount conflict resolution, price-lock semantics, multi-shipment refund window, delivery definition
Ubiquitous language glossary is thorough and precise
Business impact and stakeholder analysis adds valuable context beyond what was required
BR-8 (30-day refund window) incorrectly marked invariant: true — this is a policy parameter, not a system integrity invariant, and is an explicit failure mode in the rubric
Did not flag the specific ambiguity about when the 15-minute reservation timer starts (checkout page load vs first item addition to checkout)
Bounded context identification lumps everything into one 'Order Management' context rather than separating 'cart and checkout lifecycle' from 'inventory management' as distinct bounded contexts

agent_output_excerpt:
# Domain Analysis — E-Commerce Order & Inventory Management

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
This encodes the core **order lifecycle** of an e-commerce platform — from cart composition through checkout, payment, and post-sale refund. The rules govern three interrelated subdomains: **inventory management** (reservation semantics), **pricing/promotions** (discount exclusivity), and **order lifecycle** (immutability after payment, refund windows).
